untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of structural breaks, ML-extracted signal confidence scores, and microstructural phenomena analysis. These are higher-order statistical/machine learning constructs not available in the allowed indicator set. Allowed indicators are limited to technical price/volume metrics (RSI, MACD, ATR, Bollinger Bands, etc.) and do not include: structural break detection algorithms, ML confidence scores, event detection mechanisms, or macroeconomic event calendars. The signal's core logic—'initiate positions upon occurrence of significant market events rather than relying on time/price sampling'—requires external event detection infrastructure and ML model outputs that cannot be expressed using only price, volume, and standard technical indicators.
translation_notes: This is a meta-strategy rule about event-driven trading vs. time-series sampling. It requires: (1) Structural break detector (e.g., CUSUM, Chow test) - not available; (2) ML signal confidence output - not available; (3) Volatility spike detection tied to macroeconomic calendar - not available; (4) Microstructure deviation metrics - not available. To operationalize this strategy, add custom indicators for: structural_break_confidence, ml_signal_confidence, event_type, macroeconomic_release_flag. Alternatively, approximate with: {hvol_20 > '0.02', adx_14 > '25'} for event detection, but this loses the core 'predictive power' logic.
